text:
Refactoring is the process of improving the internal structure of code without changing its external behavior. The main goal is to make the code cleaner, more readable, and easier to maintain. Common refactoring techniques include renaming variables for clarity, breaking large functions into smaller ones, and removing duplicate code.

Refactoring does not add new features or fix bugs directly. However, it reduces technical debt—the hidden cost of messy code that makes future changes harder and slower. By regularly refactoring, developers can prevent code from becoming difficult to work with over time. Ultimately, clean code saves time and effort in the long run.
